hypothesis: Benchmarking optimization through gradient-weighted sampling — instead of uniform trace sampling for benchmarks, weight samples by gradient magnitude and direction diversity. High-magnitude gradients with diverse directions produce more informative benchmark data than low-magnitude or repetitive patterns. This reduces benchmark noise while increasing signal-to-noise ratio.
research_question: Does gradient-weighted trace sampling produce more reliable benchmark results than uniform sampling?
expected_outcome: Establish a gradient-weighted benchmark sampling protocol that improves benchmark reliability by 30% while reducing data collection overhead
